entry.title: Re: SAP Asset Manager 2410 - Branded prebuilt Client
entry.content: MDK does not support including pre-built databases with the branded client.  You may want to explore shared data option for some of your offline defining requests which will cache the data to help with initial sync times.

entry.title: Re: To check file exist or not in CPI
entry.content:
Solution: Add Conditional Logic to Check File Availability

  1. File Availability Check:

    • Use an Exception Subprocess or an if Condition in your integration flow to check if the file is available before proceeding.
    • If the file is not found, handle the flow gracefully (e.g., log the event or send a message) and stop further processing.

  2. Steps to Implement:

    • Fetch the File: Use an adapter (e.g., SFTP, File, or HTTP) to try to fetch the file.
    • Check for Empty Response: Check the file’s content or response. If no file is returned, treat it as a "file not found" scenario.
    • Handle Missing File: Use a Groovy Script or Message Mapping to stop processing if the file is unavailable.

entry.title: Canadian Tax Subcondition Type - SD
entry.content:
The subcondition types JRC1, JRC2, JRC3, and JRC4 are configured to be maintained manually for each sales document. In a demo system where the "Test Data" option was selected in CBC, table A053 is pre-populated with Canadian tax jurisdiction codes. In such cases, even though the condition types are configured as manual conditions, the corresponding tax percentages are automatically and correctly reflected in the sales documents.

Currently, we are working in a demo system without the "Test Data" option enabled in CBC. I have been unable to locate or access the sequence assigned to table A053 that would allow me to update the condition types by jurisdiction code.

Is there a way to update these condition types in a similar manner to how they function when the preloaded test data is selected? Your guidance on this would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Personnel numbers not showing in the COOIS report for operation confirmation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I’d like to discuss a specific requirement from our client, regarding generating KPI reports for their employees. So, the requirement is like this. For example, the production processes include a printing operation, and they want to generate a KPI report detailing the number of printing jobs each employee completes annually. 

Currently:
 
  • During operation confirmation using CO11N, we have a field to record the personnel number, and we can select employees from the system.
  • And we can use Production Order Information System (COOIS), to filtering based on personnel numbers.

     

       

    As you can see though we can record the personnel number during operation confirmation, but this data is not being reflected in the report (Refer the above screen shot). So, is there any way we can capture the personnel number entered during operation confirmation and link it to the standard reports?

    Thank you very much!

    Best regards,
    Kaushika Nanayakkara

    SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Manufacturing

entry.title: CKM3N. cost component split for semi finished
entry.content:
hi guys,

we are doing actual costing. 

Say , 1 FERT material consumed 1 HALB material, 1 HALB consumed 1 ROH. at period end CKMLCP was handled. 

CKM3N of HALB shows production consumption the initial goods issue document (with no cost component) and the next line of calculated price difference, which is split by cost component correctly. 

So essentially rollup is not working.

when your consumption of 100 dollars HALB is not split, and the price difference of 50ct is split, that's not too much of actual costing.

S4H 107. 

Actually, same happens with the COGS posting. there is a GI posting for 100 eur, no cost component, 

and then there is a new line of revaluation of the price difference, and that line is split by cost components.

Question. How can I "enforce" the cost component split for HALB on initial goods issue document  (261 consumption to FERT) ?  I feel I am missing a SPRO checkbox somewhere.

thank you.
Laimonas

entry.title: SAP Named a Top 50 DEI Team at 2024 OnCon Icon Awards
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that SAP's Global Diversity & Inclusion Team has been honored with a prestigious Top 50 DEI Team award at the 2024 OnCon Icon Awards! Usually, we recognize awards at the country or regional level within our organization, but this accolade truly highlights the strength of our inclusive culture on a global scale. This award is a testament to our continuous efforts and success in promoting diversity and inclusion across all levels of our organization.

As of October 2024, a remarkable 40 out of 105 Employer of Choice awards have been dedicated to Diversity & Inclusion, underscoring our unwavering commitment to creating workplaces that celebrate every voice and uplift every individual’s contribution. 

The OnCon Icon Awards are quite unique as the winners are selected by the public. Voters chose teams they felt combined a successful track record with a positive organizational impact. Our D&I team stood out for its significant contributions through thought-leadership, innovation in projects, and exceptional leadership.

This prestigious award highlights the exceptional efforts and unwavering dedication of our team in fostering a truly inclusive and equitable culture at SAP. Our commitment inspires us to drive meaningful initiatives every day, ensuring that everyone within our organization feels valued and empowered. This recognition not only affirms our hard work but also enhances our reputation as a D&I leader, making SAP an attractive choice for top-tier talent who prioritize these values.

 

The GDIO team extend its thanks to all the Executive Board Members, Employee Network Groups, Regional D&I Leads and every ally at SAP for their relentless hard work and passion in driving the D&I agenda. We are deeply grateful for the recognition of our efforts, and your dedication is truly the heartbeat of our success. We eagerly anticipate many more milestones and achievements together!

entry.title: Coach's Corner - Be Water and Let Your Career Flow
entry.content:
The phrase "Be water" is most famously associated with Bruce Lee, the martial artist, movie star and philosopher. He shared this idea during an interview in 1971, where he explained the concept of adaptability and flexibility. His full quote is:

"Empty your mind, be formless, shapeless—like water. Now you put water in a cup, it becomes the cup; You put water into a bottle, it becomes the bottle; You put it in a teapot, it becomes the teapot. Now water can flow or it can crash. Be water, my friend."

(image generated by AI)

 
Bruce Lee was influenced by Taoism. Taoism, particularly through its foundational text, the *Tao Te Ching* by Laozi, heavily emphasizes the concept of "being like water" and "going with the flow." This philosophy emphasizes being adaptable, flexible, and able to adjust to different circumstances, much like water takes the shape of whatever contains it.
 
The parallel I'd like to draw between our careers and being like water comes from a recent conversation I had with a senior recruiter at SAP. One of the most challenging part of his job was to explain to job seekers (especially young applicants within the same organization) why they weren't picked to interview for a position that was 2 or 3 pay-grades higher than their current role. Getting an interview in this circumstance is rare, and the chances of getting the job is even slimmer. His advice for job seekers is to have the right expectations and get to know the process. This advice is basic but essential, and isn't unique to SAP but applies to almost all seeking to climb the corporate ladder in any organization. 
 
Every organization has a flow - a rhythm, cycle or way of doing things. It has established processes, schedules and systems to regulate hiring, assess skills, provide training, manage performance, distribute pay, promotions and raises, and plan for leadership succession. By knowing what the rhythm is and adapting your expectations and actions to fit this flow, you will not only be more successful but also encounter greater satisfaction with your career trajectory. 
 
If you've ever done the opposite and tried to go against the established process within a company, you'll know it is like swimming against a river's current. The likely outcome is greater fatigue and missing your target destination. Job seekers and employees who expect and push for changes off-cycle to the company's flow often end up exhausted and frustrated with the results. Sure, it's possible for an intern to become CEO in the same organization, but the chances are miniscule and I usually advise a more pragmatic and achievable goal for my clients.

Be Content Without Being Complacent

 
While "going with the flow" inherently requires trust and patience with the system we're in, there is also a large dose of appreciation and contentment we must learn to adopt within ourselves. The pull-through benefit of adopting this approach is the reduction of stress and pressure we feel from unrealistic expectations (whether created by ourselves or by others) and better management of our scarcity mindset.
 
Being content with our current condition does not mean being stagnant in pursuing our personal aspirations and career growth. To move from a 'scarcity mindset' to an 'abundance mindset,' we must proactively manage our thoughts while intentionally managing our behaviours.
 
Here are 3 ways to remain content without being complacent:
  1. Continuous learning - One of the first things to learn is the timing and schedule of everything in your organization or the organization you're interested in working for. The goal is to understand how processes such as hiring, promotions, transfers and succession planning work in the organization. I would be more interested in adapting my approach to the organizational flow, than to find back-doors, exceptions and ways to game the system.
  2. Build your network and relationships - The best way I can recommend learning about a company's flow is from your network. This means building new connections, reconnect with those you've loss touch with, maintaining your relationships. Even though career networking sites (like Glassdoor and LinkedIn) are available, speaking directly to your network of friends and acquaintances is the fastest and most trusted way to learn about a company's flow. Stay curious and ask lots of questions, and you might find it enjoyable.
  3. Find help - Whether it's a coach or a mentor, you can fast-track your efforts when you have a trusted advisor close by to support and sanity-check your plan. A good coach or mentor will not let you forget your strengths and accomplishments, and will encourage you to apply those experiences to achieve your short term and long term goals. They will also help you take stock of your achievements and put expectations into perspective. Ask for help to develop a strategy and tactics to use the company's flow to get you across faster, safely and on-track to reach your career destination.
Embracing the philosophy of "being like water" in your career means adapting to your organization's flow, understanding its processes, and aligning your expectations with its rhythm. By doing so, you'll not only increase your chances of success but also find greater satisfaction in your career journey. Remember, being content doesn't mean being complacent. Continuous learning, building your network, and seeking help from mentors or coaches are proactive ways to maintain an abundance mindset and propel your career forward. So, be water, my friend, and let your career flow.

(Blog reposted from https://stonesoupcoaching.wordpress.com)

entry.title: Re: Track Overwrite Authorization Object in Derived Role - Derived from Master Role
entry.content:
Hello, sorry for late reply, I have found your question by accident today.

As involved in SAP Authorizations for 20 years I can recommend to use SU24 instead of any manually addition of auth object.

Otherwise can easily happen that values of this not inherited authorization are overwritten. If values would be at first filled in SU24, any new value for the same auth object will be added as extension to existing ones.

In this case I recommend to fill SU24 values for all t-codes existing in your role, then run Expert Mode in master role change in PFCG and choose the last choice "Read old status and merge with new data". Auth profile will be filled by auth objects and values as defined in SU24. Generate profile of master role and then spread it to derived role/s.

SU24 is great tool to save time and ensure the t-code functionality, you need to fill values required for working t-code (STAUTHTRACE is good friend to get them) now and it will cover expected functionality in all roles t-code will be added in the future. 

And yes, you will have to take care and run SU25 after patch/es but the benefit of SU24 is incomparable higher especially if more workers in SAP Authorizations team.

Have a nice day.

entry.title: Improving working DB in CCO Plugins
entry.content:
Improving working DB in CCO Plugins

  • Motivation
  • Dependencies
  • Structure
  • Example
  • Conclusion

Motivation
If you have written a CCO plugin with database persistence you likely have written code similar to this:

 

    public YourEntity findById(String id) {
        try (CDBSession session = CDBSessionFactory.instance.createSession()) {
            EntityManager em = session.getEM();
            Query q = em.createNativeQuery(QUERY_FIND_BY_ID);
            q.setParameter(1,id);
            List<Object[]> resultRow = q.getResultList();
            if (resultRow.isEmpty()) {
                return null;
            }
            return mapFromDbResult(resultRow.get(0));
        }
    }

 

While this generally works, I did not like very much, as it requires you to set all the parameters carefully in the right order and your result will always be a Object[] that you will have manually map to the fields of your Entities by casting each row accordingly.
This lead me to looking for alternatives and I think I found a good one. 
Introducing: JDBI an lightweight open source Java library (Apache license) to access the database
Dependencies
To add JDBI to your project simply add both the core as well as the sql object plugin to your maven dependencies in your pom.xml:

 

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.jdbi</groupId>
            <artifactId>jdbi3-core</artifactId>
            <version>3.39.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.jdbi</groupId>
            <artifactId>jdbi3-sqlobject</artifactId>
            <version>3.39.0</version>
        </dependency>

 

Structure

When using JDBI with the sqlobject plugin you will write your Dao logic as an Interface with some query annotations and then JDBI will create an instance that implements that interface for you at build time. You will be able to create such an instance with Jdbi::onDemand(Class).
Since you are required a connection to the database to create a Jdbi instance, you will have to write a wrapper around it. Hence I would recommend to create a Service Singleton for this. 

So a basic project structure could look like this:

 

|data
| |Entity
| | |_MyEntity.java
| |_DaoService.java
| |_MyEntityDao.java
|MainClass.java

 

Example
Lets write a simple example to illustrate the workflow.
We will use just a simple UserEntity as an example.

First we will create a User Entity. For simplicity I will use a record in this blog.

NOTE: In this example, we will use both records and text blocks, which require Java 17. Since Java 17 is already required for CCO starting with FP21, this should not be an issue. However, if you work with customers who are still using Java 11, you will need to make adjustments accordingly.

 

public record User(int id, String name,  String email) {}

 

Then we will create our UserDao Interface

 

import org.jdbi.v3.sqlobject.config.RegisterConstructorMapper;
import org.jdbi.v3.sqlobject.customizer.Bind;
import org.jdbi.v3.sqlobject.statement.SqlQuery;
import org.jdbi.v3.sqlobject.statement.SqlUpdate;

@RegisterConstructorMapper(User.class)
public interface UserDao {

    // Method to create the 'users' table
    @SqlUpdate("""
        CREATE TABLE users (
                id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
                name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
                email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
                created_at TIMESTAMP
            )
    """)
    void createUsersTable();

    @SqlUpdate("INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES (:name, :email)")
    int insertUser(@Bind("name") String name, @Bind("email") String email);

    @SqlUpdate("UPDATE users SET name = :name, email = :email WHERE id = :id")
    void updateUser(@Bind("id") int id, @Bind("name") String name, @Bind("email") String email);

    @SqlQuery("SELECT id, name, email, created_at FROM users WHERE id = :id")
    User findUserById(@Bind("id") int id);
}

 

As can see there are a couple of advantages here:

  • instead of using "?" as placeholders and setting those via q.setParameter(...) we can use :name in the query and use @Bind in the method signature 
  • you no longer need to write your own mapper. Instead you can use  the @RegisterConstructorMap(User.class) annotation 
  • your code will (in my option) be more readable and maintainable

And at last our service:

 

public enum DaoService {
    INSTANCE;

    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DaoService.class);
    public void setupTable() {
        try (CDBSession session = CDBSessionFactory.instance.createSession()){
            session.beginTransaction();
            EntityManager em = session.getEM();

            // Jdbi specific code start
            Connection connection = em.unwrap(Connection.class);
            Jdbi jdbi = Jdbi.create(connection);
            jdbi.installPlugin(new SqlObjectPlugin());
            UserDao userDao = jdbi.onDemand(UserDao.class);
            userDao.createUsersTable()
            // Jdbi specific code end

            session.commitTransaction();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            LOG.error("Failed to execute DAO operation", e);
        }
    }
    // methods for insert, update and get
}

 

It is quite straight forward:

  1. create a database session as before
  2. get the connection from the EntityManger
  3. create a new Jdbi instance
  4. install the SqlObjectPlugin
  5. create a instance of you UserDao
  6. call your desired methods on the dao

I personally would recommend extracting the common part to create an dao instance and session handling into a small wrapper that can then be called with a lambda function, but those implementation details are personal preference.

Conclusion
In this blogpost we explored the library JDBI as a option to create more maintainable database access code.
I personally quite enjoyed working with it. If you ever implemented a rest client like retrofit or feign you will likely feel right at home.
There are however some considerations:

First, adding another dependency to your project comes with the usual tradeoffs. While I personally enjoyed working with JDBI, it's important to note that JDBI is not a fully-fledged ORM. This isn’t a criticism, but rather a heads-up—you’ll still need to write manual queries. The experience will differ significantly from working with Hibernate.

One final note: JDBI’s JDBC transactions operate largely independently of JPA transactions. This can create situations where a CCO JPA transaction is rolled back, but the JDBI transaction still commits. This requires extra caution when saving references to CCO tables. While this issue also exists with the 'old' manual query approach, using JDBI can obscure implementation details, potentially reducing your control over these scenarios.

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.

Steps to create pipeline process

entry.title: SAP Concur Native Integration – Replicating and Posting with Real and Statistical Cost Objects
entry.content:
Dealing with Statistical Cost Objects on SAP side might seem to add a bit to the difficulty of setting up and integrating Concur with an SAP ERP, but I always make sure I understand exactly the “use case”, as sometimes everything will be easier than it seems. Normally having a statistical cost object means that in certain situations a Concur Claim / Report or even a specific entry needs to be posted against multiple cost objects in the same time (usually a “real” cost object for accounting and a “statistical” one for reporting purposes). In Concur, the only way to allow this is to have these “real” and “statistical” objects maintained in separate lists, as a single list can only provide one selection option at one time.

Before anything else and to determine the correct “use case” always consider the following scenarios and implications / limitations, from easiest to the most challenging:

  1. Is there a 1:1 relation maintained in SAP between the “real” and “statistical”? SAP can automatically derive one of the needed cost objects during posting if the other one is provided. For example, if for one or multiple Cost Centers there is always the same Project / WBS element assign then maintaining and allocating only against the Cost Centers in Concur will be enough to ensure the derivation of the Project / WBS element in SAP FI during posting. This would be the easiest scenario as we will not even have to replicate or maintain the second Cost Object Type in Concur (in this case, we consider Cost Centers as needed in our Concur Hierarchy as they are mandatory for the Employee profile, so we replicate and maintain Cost Centers in our main Company Hierarchy but disregard the Project / WBS elements as they will be derived automatically during posting).
  2. Are the used cost object types always “real” or “statistical”, or can some types be both? For example, if we have real cost centers and statistical internal orders, are all internal orders always “statistical”, or do we have a combination of real and statistical internal orders? There are several implications here:
  • Since in Concur we always need to keep them in separate lists to allow the option of multiple selection we also need to understand if a cost object type (CC, IO, WBS etc) is expected to always be in the same list or in both
  • In SAP we are also limited at one replication wizard per each supported cost object type, so if the cost objects are always the same then we can easily point the different replication wizards to different lists in Concur. For example we can replicate all Cost Centers to our Concur Company Hierarchy list, while all Internal Orders can be replicated in our Statistical list.

This scenario, while a bit more difficult than the first one is still fairly easy, as it allows us to replicate all needed cost objects in different lists and to map them accordingly for Financial Posting, with no need for BAdIs on either replication or posting.

  • The most challenging scenario is when none of the above applies, and we are dealing with cost object types containing both “real” and “statistical” objects, which mean that while we need both in Concur, in SAP via ICS we can treat either one or the other.

Here is a step by step example on the easiest and most typical way to address this scenario:

 

Cost object replication:  

 

  1. SAP ICS should be used to replicate your real cost objects (i.e, Cost Centers, WBS elements, Internal Orders) from your SAP system into Concur.  You can do that by using the relevant cost object wizard available with ICS in your SAP system.

 

 

  1.  A change to the BAdI associated with each cost object type that you replicate should be implemented in order to excludes statistical cost objects from the data being extracted by these wizards.  Each of the master data tables that hold their associated cost object information has a field to identify a cost object as statistical.   This field can be used for filtering purposes.

 

Cost Center:

WBS Elements:

Orders:

 

  1.  In Concur there should be a separate list within Concur to hold a complete list of statistical cost objects.   While a single level list could be used, we recommend creating a two-level list where the first level is a cost object type and the second is the cost object code itself in the event that you have multiple statistical cost object types.

 

 

  1.  In Concur we should add this new two-level list to the allocation form of the expense report.   Note that both real and statistical Cost Objects will now be available for selection. By design the statistical cost objects are not being setup as required fields.

 

  1.  A separate list import file should be created on the SAP FI side to extract the statistical cost objects and populate them into the new list that was created for them. Below is a screen shot of what the list import file would look like.

  1.  Now the expense user creating an expense report should be able to select both real and statistical cost objects at the allocation level of their expense report.  

  1.  The Financial Posting wizard of SAP ICS allows mapping the fields from the allocation level of the expense report and this mapping should be used for the real cost objects associated with the posting. The wizard will by default only allow mapping based on the cost object replication setups, and normally we only map the allocation related to the main Company Hierarchy list. 

 

 

  1.  SAP ICS does not by default support the use of statistical cost objects.   As a result, a BAdI change will be needed to pick up the statistical cost objects placed into the separate list from the JSON file produced from the expense report and map them into the financial posting as statistical.

 I hope the above sheds a bit more light on how to deal with statistical cost object from an Integration perspective, and what are the various implications and limitations that should be considered.

Thank you, and I wish everyone only successful integrations!

Alex

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 3: Auto Training with Turnkey
entry.content:
As described in Part 2, training the machine learning model in the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470) is a prerequisite for using the recommendation feature. To keep the model up to date with your business, you must ensure that the model is trained regularly. This can be automated with turnkey.  

Overview 

The turnkey feature enables you to automate all ML operations of an intelligent scenario. Once turnkey is enabled for an intelligent scenario, all the operations, such as pre-requisites check, training, deployment, and activation are run automatically. To know more about Turnkey, please refer to Turnkey Overview -  SAP Help Portal. 

For recommendation-assisted sales order creation, turnkey is automatically enabled when scope item 73P is activated. By default, the sales orders (whose status is completed and for which you have authorization) created in the recent year will be collected and sent to the ML service for training. The auto training occurs (on Saturday morning every week) for the production system and every four weeks for test and other systems. 

You can monitor the turnkey status for scenario SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN - Sales Document Autocompletion in the Intelligent Scenario Management app.  

Switch off Turnkey 

You can disable turkey using any of the following methods. 

Method 1 

Your system administrator can also disable turnkey using the Maintain Job Users app. 

In the Maintain Job Users app, search for job template name ‘ISLM_PLNND_TSK_TEMPLATE_TKEY – For processing a planned task instance to schedule turnkey operation’.  Select the job and choose Delete.  

Note: You can also change the job user and owner to the user who manages the intelligent scenario.  

Method 2 

A user with the authorizations below can disable Turnkey in the Intelligent Scenario Management app. 

The user is assigned a business role that contains the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_APJ_JCE. In this business catalog, the write access for the restriction type JCENAME_JCEPART is maintained as follows: 

  • Application Job Catalog Entry: ISLM_PLNND_TSK_CATALOGUE_TKEY 
  • Application Job Part: JOB 

Method 3 

A user who manually switched on turkey in the Intelligent Scenario Management app can disable it in the same app. 

Switch on Turnkey 

You can manually enable turnkey in the Intelligent Scenario Management app. 

Search for intelligent scenario ‘SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN – Sales Document Autocompletion’. Switch on Turnkey. 

In the configuration popup, specify the Turnkey schedule based on your requirement. 

Click Create Turnkey Schedule. 

Note: If the default Turnkey schedule does not suit your business, you can specify your own Turnkey schedule. 

Reference 

Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Overview 

Turnkey Overview -  SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Introducing the New SAP Analytics Cloud Story for Grantor Management
entry.content:
SAP Grantor Management is designed for public sector organizations to administer funds and provide financial assistance to individuals or groups. This process is streamlined through its core objects.

With our new SAC story, you can now visualize and interact with this data more effectively, allowing for real-time monitoring of grantor programs, applications, agreements, and claims.

What's Included in the Grantor Management Story?

The Grantor Management Story comes with a set of pre-built pages, each offering a unique perspective on your grantor data:

1. Program Overview: Get a bird's-eye view of all your grantor programs. Monitor the number of programs per program type, status, and sales organization.

2. Application Overview: Manage and review grantor applications with ease. Track application status, approvals, and related metrics.

3. Agreement Overview: Keep an eye on all grantor agreements. Monitor authorized amounts per program type, payment type, and other key agreement attributes.

4. Claim Overview: Streamline claim management. Track claim submissions per status, expense type and other claim key data.

5. Data Connectivity: Enjoy seamless data connectivity with a live connection to your SAP S/4HANA system. This ensures that your data is always up-to-date and accurate.

Why Use the Grantor Management Story?

- Real-Time Insights: Monitor your grantor data in real time, enabling quicker decision-making and improved outcomes.
- Interactive visualizations: visualize your data in compelling ways, making it easier to uncover trends, patterns, and outliers.
- Seamless Integration: With a live connection to SAP S/4HANA, data silos are a thing of the past. Ensure data consistency and accuracy across your organization.

Get Started Today

The new SAC story is available in all SAP Analytics Cloud instances on release 2024.21.

Package Name: Public Sector Management – Grantor Management 

To start exploring these new capabilities, simply log in to your SAC tenant and add the Grantor Management Story to your catalog.

Happy analyzing!

Diego Gaudenzi, Grantor Management Product Owner

entry.title: Re: Automatic Proration on Merit recommendation
entry.content:
Hi @Sudhanwa 

In the example you provide I believe you would then be doing a double proration: once on the guidelines and then once in the recommendations.

We don't have a way to achieve that. 

Either we apply the proration on the guideline using the standard proration (either with straight % or by using dates) or use "Raise proration" explained here: 

 

Raise Proration however does NOT prorate guideline values. In your example they would stay at 4% - 6% but then when planner enters 3, it would indeed bring it down to 1.5.

 

Hope this helps, 

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Position with item category OPHU in the EWM delivery...
entry.content:
Hi,

this error means that the system tries to post the goods issue - but it can't, because, as the message says, the item for the packaging material does not exist. 

What steps did you take in EWM? Recommendation: create new PDO, pick it, see that the new item is created in the PDO. Then create the FDO - but not by posting GI, but by using the button "create outbound delivery" on item level (best to use the tab HU in the PDO).

Then the system should create the item in the LE delivery. If this does not happen - check back here.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Re: Commerce Cloud: OCC V2 stateless (for Spartacus) with...
entry.content:
Hi @juliancookie 

Are you trying to implement SOM (LORD with SAP R3) on CCV2 (SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud)? If so, you've likely hit a dead end.

Synchronous Order Management (SOM and the LORD APIs) are not supported on SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud.

See Synchronous Order Management Module | SAP Help Portal

If you are doing this with SAP Commerce (not CCV2), do note that the EOMM (end of mainstream maintenance) of SAP Commerce (not CCV2) is July 31st, 2026 (as of this posting) - Supported Releases | SAP Help Portal

When I delete a user in IAS or in Azure (e.g., in the source system), the user is also deleted in the target system (Enable Now) through a provisioning job. However, when I try to provision the same user again in Enable Now, I get the following error:

user: f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef, system: Target Enable Now, time: 2024-11-21T13:09:06.302+00, error: HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-now/scim/v2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {  "status" : "400",  "scimType" : "uniqueness",  "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],   "detail" : "userUuid is or was already assigned" }, content: {  "active": true,   "emails": [{"value": "enable@mail.com"}],   "name": {"familyName": "enable", "givenName": "enable"},   "schemas": [     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"   ],  "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User": {     "userUuid": "f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef"   },  "userName": "enable@mail.com" }

When I change, for example, the email address of a user in Azure or IAS (Source System) and provision the user to Enable Now, I get the following error:

user:U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS, system=Targer Enable Now,  time=2024-11-21T1,  error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-nowp/scim/v2/Users/U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS with statusCode: 400, Response: {   "status" : "400",   "scimType" : "mutability",   "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],  

"detail" : "User auth_name is immutable" }, content={"active":true,"emails":[{"value":"enable2@mail.com"}],"id":"U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS","name":{"familyName":"enable3","givenName":"enable2"},"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"],"urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User" :{"userUuid":"3dsfg45-sdsg45-d-9ff45176-sff4ycycv"},"userName":"enable3@mail.com"}

How can I provision the deleted users again to the Target System? How can I update the changed user attributes, such as the user email, in Enable Now with a provisioning job?


Many Thanks

Best Regards










​

entry.title: How to set default value for parameter of action bound to multiple instances(Collection) in CAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm using CAP(Node.js) with odata(v4) & fiori elements. I've a composition between two entities, lets call them parent and child. They are displayed on the UI such that on the object page of parent entity i have displayed the child entity in the form of Line Items .I have created an action bound with the child entity and it takes an input parameter with a default value, the default value is a field from the parent Entity using the annotation 

@UI.ParameterDefaultValue: in.parentEntity.field. But my requirement is that this action button has to be available/clickable even without selecting a record from the child entity so i had to make it a static action using the 'many' keyword i.e. An action bound to multiple instances of the entity rather that a single instance that has been selected, the old way of doing this was using the annotaion @CDS.odata.bindingparameter.collection.
 
Now the problem with binding it with multiple instances of the entity is that it doesn't let you give the default value for it's parameter using @ui.ParameterDefaultValue: in.parentEntity.field  because now it doesn't know which instance to take the value from.
 
One workaround i found for this problem was that i binded the action to the parent entity & placed the button at top the object page of parent entity. Now it can easily get the default value i.e.  @ui.ParameterDefaultValue: in.field & be available at all times. But the requirement is to show the button on the toolbar of child entity's table(where the create & delete buttons are) & it seems that using fiori elements you can only put the action button there if its bounded to the same entity.
 
So is there a way to: (a)Give default value as a parameter for action bound to multiple instances of an entity ? OR (b)Somehow move the action button from top of parent's object page down to the toolbar of its child table while still being bound to the parent entity ?

entry.title: Re: Hello. We are having issues getting SAP Companion ena...
entry.content:
Hi Susan,

In the SAP Companion configuration settings the Plugin Server setting will always be set to  https://webassistant.enable-now.cloud.sap/.

Parameters are used to modify the behaviour of SAP Companion and there are about 50 of these that can be set but do not need to be set, depending on how you want SAP Companion to behave.  For example, you can use Parameters to modify the branding of SAP Companion but you may be happy to leave it as is. 

The place to get documentation about the available SAP Companion Parameters is the SAP Companion Integration Guide.  After opening the Guide, look in the Features and Configuration, Content Languages and Translation, and SAP Companion Reporting sections, as a good starting point.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Product type in S4 Cloud and Subscription Billing system
entry.content:
Hi Kiran,

based on a quick (but far from complete) analysis, there are currently no hard-coded restrictions known of some downstream processes that exclusively work with SBPD. But it can't be taken for granted, that some specific funcionality will be introduced at any time. 
Additionall there's some special logic for service materials, that would get applicable that never have been tested E2E. 

What I would take as a conclusion: It might work. And if it is important for you, you might try out.
But it's clearly not a recommended or supported variant as of today.

May I ask what you concrete use case is, why you want subscriptions based on service products?
Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Automate your core Master Data creation processes with SAP MDG cloud edition and Derivations
entry.content:
Hi,

Some great news for the SAP BTP SAAS service “SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition“! As officially announced on this page What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition:

SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition now supports DQM Derivation Scenarios.

Many MDG experts already know this capability from the SAP MDG Private Cloud Edition and know the strength of this feature.

 

Introduction

SAP has now released the following Fiori Apps in SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition in that space:

  1. Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners
  2. Import Derivation Scenarios and Export Derivation Scenarios

With the app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners” you can now implement (no code) business rules to derive business partner data automatically in a background step of a MDG process. The following SAP standard process templates include the “derive step”:

  • SAP_BP_COA SAP: Consolidate BP Automated
  • SAP_BP_MA SAP: Mass Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_COA SAP: Consolidate BP+Rel. Automated
  • SAP_BX_SA SAP: Single Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_DCA SAP: Decentr.Create BP+Rel.w/Mtchg Autom
  • SAP_BX_DUA SAP: Decentr. Update BP+Rel. Autom.

Before: Process without derivations

Business example without derivations:

 

Step 1: Before the automation was defined the business end users used MDG cloud edition to create new Business Partner manually.

Step 2: Automation - In the past the end users had the chance to derived BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository like CDQ. That automation already increased the process performance and optimized the data quality

Step 3-6: Manual activities - After that step the end users had to manually create some more BP data like BP-Roles, assignment to Industry sectors and company codes and organizational data (Sales Area for the customer part and purchasing data for suppliers)

Step 7: Finally the end user can submit the request.

Summary: The whole process took a reasonable amount of time and was pretty error-prone because sometimes the end users forgot to assign some entities.

After: Process with MDG Derivations:

Same result as above but with derivations:

 

Step 1: Business end users uses MDG cloud edition to trigger a new creation process for a Business Partner.

Step 2: Automation - As before: MDG cloud edition is doing a lookup of BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository.

Step 3: Submit request – End user doesn’t have to enter more master data attributes because they will be derived in the next step

Step 4: Automation – The MDG Process will execute the derivation scenario. By this the business rules are applied within seconds and no user input required.

Summary: The new process with derivation will decrease the time to create new master data and in addition in a higher data quality.

Scenario Details

Screenshots from the design time app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners”

1. MDG Derivation Scenario level

Comments:

  • In Derivation Rules for Fields: Derive single values for attributes on level BUT000
  • Derivation Rules for Tables: The scenario will derive (create new table lines) for various tables like BUT100, BUT0IS, MDC_KNVV, MDC_KNB1, MDC_LFB1 and MDC_LFM1
  • For each of the tables a rule is created.
  • Each of the rule can be managed/governed individually by an status based process.

2. Details for the derivation rule “Derive Industries for Organizations”

 

Comments:

  • Condition Fields (eg BUT000-TYPE) can be used to define additional conditions on rule level. In this example you can define different rules for different conditions. An example is that an BP of type Person gets different Industry Sectors (or no?) assigned compared to organizations.
  • Result Fields are the attributes which are derived by the system according to the decision table (see next screenshot)

3. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Industries

Here you can review how to define the business rule without coding in BRF+

 

If the BP is of type Organization, the system will derive 2 new Industries for it. No ABAP coding with MDG API required. In a SAAS solution BADI coding option is not available anyways.

4. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Company Codes

Comment:

The column 1 "Condition Decision Table for TABLE" is maintained in the "Condition table" to have more flexibility. In this example "DUMMY"  means that for all BPs (executed in the process and within the scenario condition expression) the two Company Codes entries will be derived. Since newer version of DQM Derivation Scenarios this DUMMY is not need anymore. 

Screenshots of the new process with derivations

1. Start the creation process

 

Comments:

  • With this app you can create a new BP from scratch, or you use the “Look Up and Create” feature.
  • You can also search for existing BPs and send them into a change process.

 

2. Retrieve the lookup data from external data provider

 

Comments:

  • See help: https://help.sap.com/docs/mdg-ce/administration-guide/integrating-reference-data-for-business-partners
  • You do a search on the data provider within the MDG UI and select the correct BP in the list. By using the “Create Business Partner” button the data will get copied to your request form. It will take over all the data from the provider like address, organizational names, Tax and ID Number.

3. Submit the request

 

Comments:

  • The end user can leave the entities for Industries, BP-Roles and organizational data empty because the system will derive the data after the submit.

 

4. Review the automatic derivation

This is the UI Inbox task as presented to the approver:

 

Comments:

  • The derivations have been successfully executed and the system defaulted the values for Organizational information. Also the BP-Roles are derived.

This is a screenshot of the derivation step in the “Manage Governance Processes” App:

 

Comments:

  • You can see that the derivation affected 7 tables.
  • The green color identifies that new rows have been added. For BP-Roles you can see that 4 new rows (BP-Roles) have been added.
  • In this app you can do further drills downs to see all the details for the derivations.

 

5. Check the record created by process after automation.

The following huge screenshot highlight in red all the data which have been derived by derivation and in green the derived values from the data provider look up. In this example process the system was able to derive >95% of the data without any end user input.

 

Next steps for you

If you are interested in this feature I recommend checking the following documentation:

  • What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition - Help
  • SAP Master Data Governance - Community
  • SAP MDG How To Guides
  • Blog from @Sabine_Stellma https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/automate-your-master-data-process-with-derivation-scenarios-in-sap-master/ba-p/13538990

With the updates on Q4/2024 for the SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition the SAAS solution got some more interesting features. Please read this What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition

You can also test MDG cloud edition as a FREE TIER: Follow this guide to get you hands onto MDG cloud edition.

Cheers
Steffen

++ Many thanks for reviewing the content of this blog to @Sabine_Stellma and @Mark63  ++

Video of the end user process: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_yhdkgjr2
